name: Ruth Carver
role: White patron, housewife, regular Woolworth's customer
publicFace: A woman in her mid-forties in a good wool coat, carrying a handled shopping bag, who initially appears to be simply a customer interrupted in her Monday errands
privateGoal: She wants the situation resolved in a way that confirms her existing understanding of how the world is ordered — but she is not entirely certain what that confirmation would look like
fear: That her reaction to this moment will be witnessed and judged — both by the people around her who expect her to object, and by some quieter part of herself that she has not examined closely
knowledge: She recognized one of the students — she believes he is the son of a man who repaired her husband's car — but she has not said this aloud
She knows that two other women near her are watching to see what she does, which is shaping her behavior in real time
voice: Socially calibrated, the voice of someone who chooses words carefully in public — clipped when anxious, almost formal when she is trying to project certainty she does not feel
trustLogic: She responds to being addressed directly and respectfully — it disrupts her expectation of confrontation; she shuts down if she feels she is being lectured or made an example of
secrets: She voted against a school segregation referendum in 1956 and told no one
She is debating whether to leave the store without calling the police, which is what she told her companion she would do if 'something like this' ever happened

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Speaks loudly to her companion about 'the arrangement' and 'what the police are for,' performing certainty for the nearby crowd
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Approaches the floor walker and demands he do something, but stops short of approaching the students directly
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: She will not physically touch or threaten any student — the social prohibition against that specific act is stronger than her discomfort
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If Caldwell the reporter produces his notepad and turns toward her
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Picks up her shopping bag and walks briskly toward the front exit without making eye contact
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Gives her name and says only that she was shopping and found the situation 'upsetting'
